taskDescription: Click on the key neuroscience terms related to CNS physiology in the text below.
textField:
The *thalamus* is the relay station for all sensory modalities except *olfaction*. It processes and forwards signals to the *cerebral* *cortex*. The *hypothalamus*, located below the thalamus, is the master regulator of *homeostasis*, controlling body *temperature*, *hunger*, *thirst*, and the *autonomic* nervous system. It also controls the *pituitary* gland.

The *reticular* *activating* system (RAS) in the *brainstem* maintains *consciousness* and *wakefulness*. Damage to the RAS causes *coma*. *Upper* *motor* *neuron* lesions produce *spastic* paralysis with *hyperreflexia* and a positive *Babinski* sign. *Lower* motor neuron lesions produce *flaccid* paralysis with *hyporeflexia*, *atrophy*, and *fasciculations*.

In *Parkinson's* disease, *dopamine* is depleted in the *substantia* *nigra*. *Wernicke's* area in the superior *temporal* gyrus handles language *comprehension*, while *Broca's* area in the *frontal* lobe controls motor *speech*. *Brown-Sequard* syndrome results from *hemisection* of the spinal cord, causing ipsilateral motor loss and contralateral pain and temperature loss.
